reviews.review_description: You can tell a lot of time went into this, specially the background images. This is not a “game” where you seed a new planet in deep space in order to spread life. It’s more of an interactive story with some history, STEM, and some science fiction. You will probably finish it in about 30 minutes but it’s worth the price. Compared to some entertainment we pay for nowadays anyway. However, not a “game” in the sense you’d think.

This was one of the first apps I purchase after buying my 3s and was delighted with the MR content it provides who doesn’t want their living room to be a space ship to the stars? It is unfortunately rather short? An update with more planets would be most welcome 😊.
I also had a problem attempting to move the teleported to an area which wasnt buried in my wall … I may be missing something?
Overall I would highly recommend this experience especially at the price point it is in, well done devs more please

reviews.review_title: Love this! My most immersive experience!
reviews.review_description:
Absolutely love Astra!
Stunning, immersive, and incredibly impressive
and Ofcourse it leaves me wanting more!

Here’s my killer suggestion:
Please consider adding a free-roaming mode. I would love to freely explore the spacecraft in my own living room, moving around naturally in my play space instead of being guided through scenes.

The experience is already beautiful, but letting players roam and discover at their own pace would make it truly magical. 

Add more planets, moons, and celestial objects too 
 I mean… the sky is the limit, right?
Keep up the amazing work. This is something special

Having your room transformed into a space ship is awesome! The blend of MR and VR is pretty sick. The graphics are visually pleasing. I also like that you can play using your hands. However, I do have some critiques.

The story is WAY too short. It only allows you to travel to five destinations briefly, and four of them are from two of the same planets you travel back and forth from. Basically, all you do is stand and collect elements, and that's it. Towards the end, there's a lot more watching than there is interacting, listening to someone going on and on about the same subject, which really irritates me. I really wish the game would allow me to travel to all those other planets on the map. There's no freeplay or a way to revisit previous planets. I purchased this game on a sale, because reading other reviews, I would not buy at full price. 

In conclusion, I think the game has great potential, but it could've been better. So, would I recommend Astra? Probably not. Will I ever replay Astra? Maybe I will if I want to feel again what it's like to travel across the stars from my room.

reviews.review_title: Should have listen to the warnings
reviews.review_description: Took 10 mins, boring gameplay and story trying to sound inspiring but your're already too irritated by the time you get to the end , you only get to visit about 4 planets so you have no freedom. The surfaces of the planets did look really nice however.

Everything works for me just fine. The design of the space travel is astonishing... the mixed reality of spaceship inside my living room, encounters of different worlds, narration, interaction for finding ingredients for life.. I am positively surprised. The story is very good. Great experience.
Just seems i,ve wasted some money purchasing Astra....my room does,nt match the one required to continue into the experience...perhaps it would be better to mention this in the app description.

reviews.review_title: You need big walls, clear of clutter.
reviews.review_description: At first, I couldn’t get it to work & just had the circle that others have mentioned & I couldn’t do anything & it seems to be because I had too much “stuff” on my walls; (shelves, pictures, etc etc). I tried it in the kitchen where I have much less clutter & bigger plain walls & hey presto, it worked straight away. I hope this helps. Great app, worth the hassle. Would fly my kitchen into space again   In fact I definitely will!
No control over any of it.   Only instruction I was able to complete, was activate and deactivate the binoculars... that's it.
It’s amazing overall, great for anyone in the family. Looking forward to completing I’ve spent a couple hours so far exploring and it’s fun.
Can’t even complete the first task — not able to lift/move objects.
The story is way too short and you get to visit only 2 planets. Game needs to add the whole solar system
Doesn't work on quest 2. Shows a circle amidst your room in which you cant interact. Seeking refund

If I could get my money back, I would in a heartbeat. The visuals are so low-effort that it's almost comical. It's neat in concept, but is short, repetitive, and pretty ugly to look at. The marketing materials are very deceptive -- don't be fooled like I was!

I read the reviews so knew to expect a short interactive story like experience. Being a big space fan, I was looking forward to the apparent amazing visuals and feeling immersed like I was actually visiting these places.

Unfortunately the visuals are not amazing like I’ve seen mentioned in so many reviews, I found them quite poor. The clouds of Jupiter were almost of laughable quality for example. The water geysers on Europa looked terrible. 

Additionally I found the mixed reality experience of making your room a spaceship to be completely immersion breaking, as, well, my home is not a spaceship.

This was an amazing experience that I thoroughly enjoyed. My only complaint is that it’s perhaps not as interactive as first though, they lead you to believe you can visit more planets than you actually can. ￼

reviews.review_title: It’s amazing experience but is short
reviews.review_description:
I would like if you added a mode were you can just travel to any planet not in story 
If if this feature does come i will pay more like an add on
worlds are beautiful but the tasks are tedious and not fun. i just want to visit various worlds and see them with my own eyes.  not stand there for 10 mins a time catching elements.

This is a truly breathtaking showcase experience! If you enjoy space, Sci-fi, good graphics, spirituality, or just plain wonder, this is a must-buy! What an incredible use of Mixed Reality that truly takes you to other worlds! I honestly can't believe it's so cheap! 

The hand controls are intuitive and very well done and I was really surprised how rock solid the framing and sense of movement in the Mixed Reality was! This was clearly made as a passion project by people who care and wanted to deliver on that sense of space wonder that they've always had. I felt like a kid again, but also an adult, because I could see and FEEL my room as a spaceship! 

Really cool imagery, great use of colors and depth, epic astral-themed soundtrack, and just enough length to feel satisfied. I'll be showing this one off to as many relatives as possible. It's exactly what I wanted from a VR headset! Kudos to the team for making me feel like a stardust spirit in the best way possible. I can't wait to dream about the experience I just had...
This is clearly an educational experience  meant for kids, quite annoying and limited. Not at all what I expected given reviews. Interface is also quite clunky.

Could have been better. I was thinking I would be able to go to all nine planets and see what it would be like to stand on the surface. They only bring you to Jupiter, Jupiters moons and like a moon around Saturn. You just collect elements on the plants and then you go back to your ship. It’s a short story game.. no actual like game play. Could have been a lot better. I beat the game in like an hour… visually it was great.. but super limited what you can do

reviews.review_title: An interactive space story, not a game
reviews.review_description:
If you’re looking for a game, this is not for you.

The whole story is probably about 30-45 min long, and the only reason you’d want to replay it is to show other people, or to revisit the beautifully reimagined visual interpretations of what some of the surface of the planets look like. And to maybe learn a quick science lesson on chemical elements. 

I was actually a space geek when I was young, but the older I got, the scarier it seemed to me. So idk why I bought this. But this was actually really, really cool. From turning my home to a spaceship, to beaming onto the planet surfaces like I’m on Star Trek, to the beautiful environments - I was just oozing out loud with, “this is sooo cool” and “whoa.” My dogs were wandering around the space ship (aka my living room); it made me think how cool it was to travel with my pets lol. And every time I beamed up, I would just say to myself, “Beam me up, Scotty; I gotta go to the potty!” (if you don’t know, it’s a Star Trek thing, haha).      

It’s basically an amazing “under $10 thing” I could have spent on an interactive space story or ticket to an interactive museum. And because I’m at home, I could be as big of a kid as I wanted. Worth it.

Well I think all of the good reviews are fake. I have tried repeatedly to get this to work and it’s impossible so I get it to load no problem but a box appears that sometimes lets me open it and when it does open it’s filled with stuff that doesn’t make sense and I can’t do anything with any of it. There are no portals no open windows with planets like it shows nothing but my room? Unless you need a big house with empty rooms to use it in which I do not and if so they should tell you that before you buy it. I love outer space and sci-fi stuff and I really wanted this to work but forget it don’t waist your money and or effort.

reviews.review_title: Nice game but needs a free range mode
reviews.review_description: Great graphics and nice overall game but really was a bummer to learn that I can’t go to any of the places on the map other than the few they choose.
I’ve purchased this on my phone but it won’t let me download it to my vr set.

I bought this yesterday and no matter what I do the initial box to open is always outside outside the boundary,I'm not playing in a small room this is really disappointing was looking forward to playing this👎

this game is kind of a mix. There is some seriously good things about this game that I absolutely love, but there’s some really disappointing parts to it. So here we go.

The good :
my living room was a spaceship, with a giant window, with awesome views of the planets and moons that we visited. 
The controls were simple and intuitive, and setting a course for a moon or a planet felt like you’re in Star Trek or something. It was awesome. 

The teleporter/transporter effect is incredible, literally feels like you’re beaming down 
when you’re down on the planets, admittedly, you can’t explore much, but the visuals are stunning, and it really does feel like you’ve beamed down to another world


I also love that you use your hands instead of the controllers. Very relaxing I could literally play this game for hours and hours except….

The Bad:
when I first figured out how to play this game, I was excited, and immediately wanted to set a course for Venus. I pressed Venus over and over again, and nothing happened, it was at this point that I realized that it was a guided experience, and you cannot explore on your own. I thought no problem, I’m sure there’s an option for that. Right? Nope.  no option for that, you’re stuck with the story, and you can only visit a few places.

and when I say stuck with the story, I mean, STUCK with the story, there is no variation at all. Not even a little, you are completely guided by this girl. Which leads me to my next negative.

The narrator.  I had the volume all the way up, and you can barely hear her, she has a dry mouth like cottonmouth. You can like hear her tongue sticking to the top of her mouth.  90% of everything she says is all stuff I could look up on the Internet, or I’ve known since I was 12, and frankly, I didn’t buy this for that. I just want to go to different planets and moons beam down and walk around. I want to be able to explore the entire solar system.
which leaves me to my next negative


The content is really really really short. I mean really short. This is more like a demo.  spoiler alert, although not really because it only takes about an hour. You explore Jupiter, Europa, Titan, and enceladus. Four places. That’s it.

So, after being bummed out by all this, I played through the entire experience, thinking that maybe it would open up an option after you beat it to be able to go back and visit those places again, and I thought well at least I’ll be able to play it once in a while, or show it off to my friends or something. But guess what. There’s no option for that either.

if you want to go to Europa, you literally have to play through the entire boring experience with the boring narrator, waiting to get there. Completely guided, you can’t even stop and look around without the robot constantly telling you to get moving.


conclusion:
so much potential, but it’s wasted right now. The developer really needs to fix all of the negatives. They could charge more even, if they fix all the negatives and allowed you to actually explore the solar system at Will. Seriously guys allow an option, where you can just click on whatever planet/moon you want to go to and just allow you to go there without the narrator. it doesn’t need to be a science lesson, I just want to turn my living room into a spaceship. You gotta expand the option guys seriously I would return this if I could. As of right now.


OK, I hope the developers listen to me and take me seriously. Thanks for reading. God bless.


Pat

This, in theory, is exactly the sort of thing you want to show off to friends and family, but it's such a chore! 

What this app desperately needs is a sandbox mode. Just let us stand there at that cool console, travelling to cool worlds. 

But no, we get this galactically skippable guff with a box and a VO that I must have endured about 50 times now just to see the Earth from space.

Oh, and every single time, despite having access to all my spatial data, it puts the teleporter five feet outside my 5th floor flat window. 

If this had a simple, go into a mixed reality spaceship and look at how cool the universe is, I'd give it 5 stars. It has one cool thing, the spaceship inside your own home, and it makes it hard to enjoy that one element.

developer_response.body:
Hi TheDirector50,

We're really sorry to hear that you had issues seeing the chemical element. Could you let us know more about this at support@astreaimmersive.io ?

We'll do our best to help you out.

Thank you for letting us know about your problem.

Best regards.

reviews.review_description: Really wanted to play this but couldn't because it's not sitting friendly. Had to turn around to advance and I couldn't. Such a shame

Teleport device is positioned outside my small room and not easy to get to without breaking the immersive VR experience. There should be something in the settings to move it to a more accessible place. Would be useful for disabled people. But, there are no accessible settings to begin with. Mashing all the buttons and pushing in the thumb stick controllers does nothing. All I could do is press the right oculus menu button and quit the game.

developer_response.body:
Hi nocshift138,

Thank you for purchasing Astra and taking the time to leave us a review. 

I'm sorry you had some issues with the teleporter. Unfortunately we don't have the possibility to manually move it for now. 

However, the last update allows you to experience ASTRA in sitting mode and all objects should be placed inside your guardian. Make sure your experience is up to date, then try to make your room set up again, making sure your guardian is inside the room set up.

If you still have issues, please send us an email at support@astreaimmersive.io and we'll do our best to help you enjoy your space travel.

Best Regards,
Zoé

reviews.review_description:
Pro: 
- Got shivers in the beginning because it is so immersive.
- It's a fun and educational experience
- AR is awesome
- Music is perfectly fitting
- Great narration 

Neutral:
- It's not a game

Con:
- Story line is a bit repetitive
- Outro video should be in-engine instead of pre-rendered
- Not interesting to play it again

This is just a lazy and bad "game". It uses mixed reality ok with the room turning into a space ship, but the actual play of going to a planet and then analyzing elements is just boring, and the sights are so lame. I went to Jupiter for example expecting a really cool view, instead I got a really bad looking skybox that surrounded me with no real 3d environment, and nothing interesting to look at. I mean, come on, did you guys try to play this game before releasing it, it is sooo boring, I would refund it if I hadnt bought it in a bundle with a better game.
I can’t get it to work with my room. I’m within the time limit for a refund but can’t figure out how to do this.

developer_response.body:
Hi NosePunch,

Thank you for purchasing Astra and leaving us a review. I'm sorry you had some issues with the experience.

Please send us an email at support@astreaimmersive.io and we'll do our best to help you get everything to work correctly so you can travel into space!

Thank you very much,

Best Regards,
Zoé

reviews.review_title: More like a demo than a full release.
reviews.review_description:
This game is short (very short linear story). It may be cheap but, in my opinion, a little expensive for what you actually get.

It's like the tech demo(s) you used to get 9n the psvr-1.

developer_response.body:
Hi TroubledSoul,

Thank you for taking the time to share this review. 

I totally understand your disappointment when the experience ended sooner than you expected, and it seems like the universe we created left you wanting much more exploration—which is a sentiment we deeply appreciate. We faced some real constraints with budget and time that shaped the scope of what we could create this time around. 

We hope you understand why we priced it that way.

Thank you very much again for your review!

reviews.review_description: Can’t get it to work in my space

developer_response.body:
Hi there! 

We just released an update which might fix your issue. If it didn't, please reach out to us at support@astreaimmersive.io and we'll do our best to help you.

Have a great day,
Zoé

reviews.review_description: Every time you start the experience it buries a box needed under the floor. It’s not my setup area either, waste of money.

reviews.review_description: The box keeps spawning inside the wall matter where I go

developer_response.body:
EDIT: Hi again, we just released a new update that fixes this issue. Please reach out to us at support@astreaimmersive.io if you still have some problems.

----

Hi Thatoneastronaut, 

I'm sorry to hear you're having an issue with ASTRA. 

To help resolve this, I recommend redoing your guardian setup without any furniture, if that's an option. This can help ensure that the app recognizes a clear space where the box should appear, making it accessible for you. Also note that the app is designed to be standing so make sure you are.

If you try this and still encounter issues, please reach out to us at support@astreaimmersive.io. We're here to help make your experience with Astra enjoyable and hassle-free. Thank you for your patience and for letting us know about this problem.

reviews.review_description: This app doesn’t work

Hi bobby³!

Thank you for buying and trying ASTRA.

I'm sorry to hear you had issues. Could you send us an email at support@astreaimmersive.io with some more details? 

We'll do our best to help you so you can explore space from your livingroom and enjoy the experience!

reviews.review_description: Total waste of money

I loved it. I gave just 4 stars because I reckon that all the interactive stories should have a modality to visit the different VR environments once somebody completes the story. Otherwise, you run the story once, and that's it. Hope the programmers will add this functionality in the next upgrade.
